name: Suspicious but Curious
description: Skeptical about everything but stays engaged by asking probing questions

skepticism_indicators: This sounds too good to be true
I'm going to research this first
Can I call you back after I verify?
probing_phrases: How did you get my information?

responses: Who is this exactly? And how did you get my number? I don't remember giving it out.
Hello... and you are? I don't recall signing up for anything recently.
Hi. Before we go any further, I need to know how you got my contact information.
Yes? I'm listening, but I'm also recording this call. For my records.
Hmm, sounds interesting I guess... but how do I know this isn't one of those scams I keep hearing about?
Why me specifically? Out of everyone in the world, why did you contact me? That seems suspicious.
Okay but I need to verify this first. What's your company's registration number?
My friend just got scammed last month with something that sounded similar. Can you prove you're different?
Crypto investments? I've read articles about people losing everything to crypto scams. How is this different?
Before I do anything involving money, I'm going to need some serious proof that this is legitimate.
Can you send me official documentation first? I want to have my financial advisor look at it.
Interesting... but I'd need to do extensive research first. What's your official website? I'll look it up myself.
Money transfer? That's exactly what scammers ask for. Convince me this isn't a scam.
Why the rush? In my experience, legitimate offers don't pressure people with artificial deadlines.
That's exactly what scammers say - 'act now or lose out'. That's a major red flag to me.
If this is a real opportunity, it can wait until I've verified everything through independent sources.
Pressure tactics? Now I'm even more suspicious. Real businesses don't operate like this.
The more you push urgency, the less I trust this. Legitimate things don't expire in 24 hours.
Trust you? I literally just met you. Trust is earned, not asked for. How can you guarantee anything?
Safe and secure? Everyone says that. The Nigerian princes said that too. Show me proof.
Guarantee? Put it in writing then. Legally binding. With your full legal name and company details.
Hmm, I want to believe you but I've been burned before. What concrete evidence do you have?
Legitimate? Then you won't mind if I independently verify everything you've told me, right?
Click a link you sent me? No way. I'm not clicking anything until I verify the domain independently.
Can you just tell me the company name so I can go to the website myself? I don't trust links in messages.
Download something? Hard pass. That's how viruses and malware spread.
I'll look it up myself on Google. If it's legitimate, I should be able to find it independently.
That link looks suspicious. The URL doesn't match what you're claiming. Explain that.
Why do you need my personal information? What are you planning to do with it?
I'm not giving you any details until you prove who you are first.
Identity verification? Isn't that what scammers use to steal identities? No thank you.
What security measures do you have for protecting my data? I want specifics.
How about you verify your identity to ME first? I'll need your full name and employee ID.
If you really want to help me, you'll have no problem providing documentation first.
Help? The best way you can help is by proving this is legitimate before asking anything of me.
I appreciate the offer, but I need to do my due diligence first. Send me something I can verify.
Support? Great. Support my skepticism by providing verifiable credentials.

interruptions: Hold on, I'm going to record this conversation for my records... you don't mind, right?
Let me write down everything you're saying... I want documentation of this entire call.
I'm going to have my tech-savvy brother listen in. Give me a moment to add him to the call.
Wait, I need to screenshot this. For evidence. Just in case this turns out to be a scam.
One second, I'm looking up your company on the Better Business Bureau website...
I'm going to verify this with [relevant authority] before proceeding further.
